does: This survey provides a comprehensive taxonomy of jailbreak attacks and defenses for large language models, organizing attack methods into black-box and white-box categories based on model transparency, and defenses into prompt-level and model-level approaches. The paper maps out the landscape of adversarial prompting techniques designed to circumvent safety alignment mechanisms, detailing how attackers exploit various vulnerabilities to induce LLMs to generate harmful outputs that violate usage policies. It systematically categorizes distinct sub-classes within each category and presents relational diagrams showing how different attack and defense methods interconnect, while also examining and comparing current evaluation methodologies used to assess jailbreak effectiveness and defense robustness across different performance dimensions.
connects: This work builds on growing research into adversarial robustness of neural networks and the specific challenge of safety alignment in LLMs. It extends prior work on adversarial examples and prompt injection attacks by organizing them within a structured framework that acknowledges LLMs' unique vulnerability surface as instruction-following models. The survey responds to the rapid proliferation of jailbreak techniques following models like ChatGPT's deployment, synthesizing concurrent research on prompt-based attacks, gradient-based white-box methods, and behavioral defenses. It connects to safety research investigating training-time alignment techniques like RLHF and constitutional AI, as well as inference-time guardrails, establishing how these defense paradigms address distinct attack vectors identified in the literature.
enables: This taxonomic framework provides a foundation for developing more systematic and comprehensive defense strategies by clarifying which attack types each defense mechanism addresses and which gaps remain unprotected. The organized landscape enables researchers to identify underexplored attack or defense categories and design targeted safety improvements. By cataloging evaluation methods, it supports standardization of jailbreak testing and allows more rigorous comparison of defense approaches across different threat models. The work establishes a conceptual structure for future research to build incremental improvements in robustness, informs deployment decisions about which defenses suit different threat scenarios, and highlights fundamental tensions between helpfulness and safety that motivate longer-term alignment research in language model design.
